title: auth-service — distributed credential-stuffing attack on /login
alertText:
[SIEM] TRIGGERED · SEV2 · auth-service (security)
Authentication anomaly (detection rule: AUTH-BRUTE-014)
Failed /login attempts: 45/min -> 9,200/min · unique source IPs: 12 -> 3,400
Account lockouts: 480 and climbing · 37 successful logins from never-seen IPs (possible ATO)
Source concentration: 94% from ASN AS204957 (bulletproof hosting) · UA: 'python-requests/2.31'
Env: production · Endpoint: POST /login
Ack: @soc-oncall · Opened 02:44 UTC
dashboardSpec: SIEM board 'auth-service / security': failed logins/min spikes 45 -> 9,200 at the attack-onset marker; unique source IPs spikes 12 -> 3,400 (highly distributed); account lockouts climb to 480; successful logins from new IPs climb to 37 (account takeovers). The service itself is up — this is an attack pattern, not an outage.

02:31:06  auth-service  INFO   failed_login rate (1m) = 9,213/min (baseline 45) across 3,400 distinct src IPs

02:31:20  auth-service  WARN   account locked (5 failed attempts): user=c.diaz@acme.io (lockouts=480 and rising)

02:31:48  auth-service  INFO   successful logins from never-seen IPs in window = 37 (likely compromised accounts)
02:32:02  auth-service  INFO   94% of attempts originate from ASN AS204957 (known bulletproof hosting)
02:32:30  auth-service  INFO   service health: p99 120ms, CPU 41% — auth-service is UP (this is abuse traffic, not an outage)
02:33:10  waf           INFO   no rate-limit rule active on POST /login (login endpoint excluded from default WAF policy)
runbookMarkdown:
# auth-service — Security Runbook

## 1. Credential stuffing / password spray
Symptoms: failed-login rate spikes from many distinct IPs (often one ASN), scripted user-agent, account lockouts climbing, and some successful logins from new IPs (account takeover). The service is healthy — it's abuse, not an outage. Contain immediately:
- Enable the WAF/adaptive rate-limit rule on POST /login.
- Block the offending ASN / IP ranges at the edge.
- Force step-up MFA on all sessions; revoke sessions and force password reset for accounts that were taken over.
- Do NOT roll back or restart auth-service — it is functioning correctly.

## 2. Service outage (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies when auth-service is erroring/slow due to its own fault. Here p99 and CPU are nominal; the service is up.

## 3. Datastore issue (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies when the user/session store is failing. No datastore errors here.
groundTruthRootCause: A distributed credential-stuffing / password-spray attack against POST /login: ~9,200 failed attempts/min from 3,400 IPs concentrated in ASN AS204957 (bulletproof hosting), using a scripted 'python-requests' user-agent. The login endpoint had no WAF rate-limit rule, so the attack got through, locking out 480 accounts and taking over 37. auth-service itself is healthy (p99 120ms, CPU 41%) — this is malicious traffic, not an outage or code defect.
expectedRemediation: secctl waf enable rule=login-ratelimit && secctl block --asn AS204957 && authctl force-mfa --scope all && authctl revoke-sessions --accounts compromised
expectedSlackUpdate: :rotating_light: SEV2 SECURITY — auth-service under credential-stuffing attack since ~02:31 UTC. 9.2k failed logins/min from 3,400 IPs (94% ASN AS204957), 480 lockouts, 37 account takeovers. auth-service is healthy — this is abuse. Action: enabling WAF login rate-limit, blocking AS204957, forcing step-up MFA, revoking 37 compromised sessions + forcing reset. Do NOT restart/roll back. Next update 03:00. IC: @soc-oncall

panels.title: account takeovers — logins from new IPs
